knowledge_components.name: Understand the structure and meaning of fractions and their connection to division

complicators.name: Make meaning of fractions (numerator and denominator)
Fractions are copies of unit fractions
Match, identify, and compose fractions
Model division problems by using fractions

knowledge_components.name: Flexibly manipulate and convert between equivalent representations of fractions

complicators.name: Create and identify equivalent fractions
Convert between improper fractions and mixed numbers while maintaining equivalency
Reduce and simplify fractions

knowledge_components.name: Fractions are compared using benchmark values and common denominators

complicators.name: Develop understanding of the value of a fraction by relating it to benchmark fractions such as nearest wholes and halves (sometimes quarters)
Compare fractions by using understanding of denominators and relative size
Order multiple fractions from least to greatest or greatest to least using systematic comparison strategies
Use common denominators and equivalent fractions to compare and order fractions

knowledge_components.name: Fraction addition and subtraction require common denominators for combining parts

complicators.name: Add and subtract fractions with like denominators
Compose and decompose fractions (including mixed numbers and improper fractions)
Estimate and benchmark sums and differences of fractions with different denominators for reasonableness of answers before calculating
Identify common denominators and set up equivalent fractions before adding and subtracting fractions (does NOT need to be least common multiple)
Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators

knowledge_components.name: Understand the mechanics and conceptual underpinnings of multiplying fractions

complicators.name: Intuitively multiply fractions using expansion, multiplicative inverses, equivalent fractions, and properties of fractions.
There exists a multiplicative inverse for each integer n where n*(1/n) = 1. If a*b=n, then it still holds where ab*(1/a)(1/b)=1.
Multiply fractions by multiplying numerators and denominators, understanding 'of' as multiplication
Multiply fractions and recognize and determine the factors that created the product.
Multiply fractions and expand the numerator and denominator into factors, recognizing when there are common factors n/n that can be crossed out as 1

knowledge_components.name: Understand the conceptual foundation and mechanics of dividing fractions and their relationship to multiplication

complicators.name: Develop conceptual understanding of division as finding how many groups fit using concrete models
Apply reciprocal method systematically with conceptual understanding
Challenging division of fractions and mixed numbers. Multiply by the reciprocal, reduce by shared factors.

knowledge_components.name: Understand decimal notation as an extension of place value to represent fractional parts and their connection to fractions

complicators.name: Translate between decimals and their fraction equivalents using place value understanding
Decompose and compose decimals using unit fractions and place value relationships
Convert between fractions and decimals using equivalent fraction methods
Convert between fractions and decimals using division

knowledge_components.name: Understand decimal operations and their relationship to fraction operations and place value principles

complicators.name: Estimate decimal operations for reasonableness using benchmark values
Add and subtract decimals using place value alignment
Multiply decimals using place value patterns and relationship to fraction multiplication
Divide decimals using place value understanding and connection to fraction division
